name: [PLACEHOLDER] Atlas Underwriting Engine
one_liner: A five-milestone placeholder project exercising the moat and specifics sections.
overview:
[PLACEHOLDER] Atlas needed faster, more consistent underwriting decisions on a growing deal pipeline without adding headcount.

[PLACEHOLDER] VCP built an underwriting engine that combines structured deal data with a review workflow, cutting decision turnaround while keeping a human in the loop for every call.

timeline.title: Discovery
timeline.note: [PLACEHOLDER] Mapped the existing underwriting workflow and data sources.

timeline.title: Data model
timeline.note: [PLACEHOLDER] Defined the deal schema and scoring inputs.

timeline.title: Engine v1
timeline.note: [PLACEHOLDER] Shipped first scoring pass to a pilot group of underwriters.

timeline.title: Workflow integration
timeline.note: [PLACEHOLDER] Wired the engine into the existing review queue.

timeline.title: Full rollout
timeline.note: [PLACEHOLDER] Rolled out to the entire underwriting team.

moat: [PLACEHOLDER] Proprietary scoring model trained on a decade of Atlas's own deal outcomes — a dataset a competitor cannot replicate without the same history.

name: [PLACEHOLDER] Meridian Marketplace
one_liner: A twenty-milestone placeholder project exercising the full long-timeline and all optional sections.
overview:
[PLACEHOLDER] Meridian wanted to stand up a two-sided marketplace connecting regional suppliers with mid-market buyers, replacing a slow, manual quoting process run over email and spreadsheets.

[PLACEHOLDER] VCP designed and built the marketplace end to end — listings, quoting, payments, and post-sale logistics tracking — and handed off a fully staffed internal team to run it.

timeline.title: Opportunity sizing
timeline.note: [PLACEHOLDER] Sized the addressable supplier and buyer base.

timeline.title: Discovery interviews
timeline.note: [PLACEHOLDER] Interviewed 20 suppliers and 15 buyers.

timeline.title: Spec v0
timeline.note: [PLACEHOLDER] Drafted the initial product spec.

timeline.title: Design system
timeline.note: [PLACEHOLDER] Established the marketplace's visual and interaction language.

timeline.title: Supplier onboarding flow
timeline.note: [PLACEHOLDER] Built the supplier listing and verification flow.

timeline.title: Buyer search & quoting
timeline.note: [PLACEHOLDER] Shipped search, filtering, and quote requests.

timeline.title: Private alpha
timeline.note: [PLACEHOLDER] Launched to 5 supplier and 5 buyer accounts.

timeline.title: Payments integration
timeline.note: [PLACEHOLDER] Integrated escrowed payments for completed orders.

timeline.title: Alpha feedback pass
timeline.note: [PLACEHOLDER] Incorporated feedback from alpha cohort.

timeline.title: Logistics tracking
timeline.note: [PLACEHOLDER] Added shipment tracking and delivery confirmation.

timeline.title: Private beta
timeline.note: [PLACEHOLDER] Expanded to 30 supplier and 40 buyer accounts.

timeline.title: Trust & safety pass
timeline.note: [PLACEHOLDER] Added dispute resolution and fraud checks.

timeline.title: Beta feedback pass
timeline.note: [PLACEHOLDER] Second round of product changes from beta usage.

timeline.title: Billing & invoicing
timeline.note: [PLACEHOLDER] Automated supplier payouts and buyer invoicing.

timeline.title: Admin console
timeline.note: [PLACEHOLDER] Internal tools for the Meridian ops team.

timeline.title: Public launch
timeline.note: [PLACEHOLDER] Opened the marketplace to all suppliers and buyers.

timeline.title: Growth loop v1
timeline.note: [PLACEHOLDER] Shipped referral and repeat-order incentives.

timeline.title: Scale hardening
timeline.note: [PLACEHOLDER] Performance and reliability work ahead of peak season.

timeline.title: Internal team handoff
timeline.note: [PLACEHOLDER] Trained and transitioned to Meridian's internal team.

timeline.title: Engagement close
timeline.note: [PLACEHOLDER] VCP engagement formally closed; marketplace fully owned by Meridian.

moat: [PLACEHOLDER] Two-sided network effects plus exclusive integration with Meridian's existing supplier contracts — new entrants would need to rebuild both sides of the market from zero.
business_model:
